title: Multi-tenant SaaS
scenario: Build an optionally authenticated multi-tenant SaaS with threat modeling, RLS, Kratos identity, Gate authentication, Keto authorization, Forge/Fabric isolation, BYOK provider keys, anonymous-mode decisioning, and tenant-boundary proof.
termination: Stop only when cross-tenant access fails at API and database boundaries, BYOK references are tenant-scoped and encrypted or externally secret-managed, realtime events are isolated, anonymous mode is explicitly accepted or disabled, and deployment manifests contain no inline secrets.

prompts.prerequisites: Verify tenancy model, threat model owner, Postgres/RLS strategy, secret manager target, optional Kratos/Gate/Keto decision, Forge/Fabric availability, and BYOK provider list.
prompts.discovery: Read auth/security patterns, deployment catalog, Forge/Fabric/Gate docs, Kratos/Keto docs, database policies, tenant identity model, and current app configuration.
prompts.feynman: Explain tenant isolation from request identity through API, RLS, realtime channels, BYOK secret access, audit logs, and anonymous mode.
prompts.kbd_assess: Assess tenants, roles, data classes, BYOK risks, anonymous access, realtime scope, audit needs, onboarding, and deployment environments.
prompts.kbd_analyze: Analyze existing auth adapters, database schema, RLS policies, secret references, Forge/Fabric isolation, Gate/Kratos/Keto integration points, and deployment overlays.
prompts.kbd_spec: Specify threat model, tenant IDs, identity mapping, authorization tuples, RLS, BYOK storage references, realtime isolation, audit events, anonymous-mode decision, and tenant-boundary tests.
prompts.kbd_plan: Plan identity and tenant types first, then RLS, API enforcement, BYOK secret references, realtime isolation, UI settings, deployment manifests, tests, critic, and retention.
prompts.research: Use official Ory, Kubernetes, Postgres, cloud secret manager, and local Flint project sources to confirm current integration behavior and configuration constraints.
prompts.implementation: Implement tenant identity as a required domain type. Do not pass raw provider keys through logs or public docs. Make anonymous mode an explicit product decision, not a default accident.
prompts.public_boundary_verification: Attempt cross-tenant reads/writes through public API, database policy, realtime subscription, and BYOK access. Render deployment manifests and scan for inline secrets.
prompts.independent_critic: Review threat model coverage, RLS proof, auth/authorization boundaries, BYOK handling, Forge/Fabric isolation, anonymous mode, deployment secrets, and audit evidence.
prompts.reflection_retention: Record security decisions, tenant-boundary failures, redactions, manifest proof, and reusable SaaS skill/template improvements.
prompts.recovery: If tenant isolation fails, stop feature work and repair the identity/RLS model before UI or deployment expansion.
prompts.stop_conditions: Stop for missing threat model, unscoped BYOK keys, inline secrets, ambiguous anonymous mode, unverified RLS, cross-tenant leak, or direct production deployment.
